Operation 'listalltickets' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_context. Original description lacks detail about input parameters (pagination controls) and output structure. Summary is generic. Enhanced version specifies pagination in summary and explains what parameters are accepted and what Ticket object properties are returned, improving AI agent ability to determine when to use this operation and how to process results.

data.current_description: Returns all tickets stored in the database with pagination support.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves all tickets from the system with pagination support. Accepts parameters for page number, page size, and sorting options. Returns a paginated list of Ticket objects containing ticket details such as ID, subject, status, priority, and timestamps. Useful for displaying ticket inventories or bulk operations.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks detail about input parameters (pagination controls) and output structure. Summary is generic. Enhanced version specifies pagination in summary and explains what parameters are accepted and what Ticket object properties are returned, improving AI agent ability to determine when to use this operation and how to process results.

message: Operation 'submitticket' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Summary is clear and actionable. Description is too vague about inputs and outputs. Enhanced description specifies what fields are expected in the request, what the response contains (newly created Ticket with ID), and side effects (ticket becomes available for assignment). This helps AI agents understand the complete lifecycle of ticket creation.

data.current_description: Creates a new ticket in the support site.
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new support ticket in the system. Accepts ticket details including subject, description, contact information, and optional priority/category. Returns the newly created Ticket object with assigned ticket ID and initial status. The ticket is immediately available in the system and can be assigned to support staff. Requires appropriate authentication.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and actionable. Description is too vague about inputs and outputs. Enhanced description specifies what fields are expected in the request, what the response contains (newly created Ticket with ID), and side effects (ticket becomes available for assignment). This helps AI agents understand the complete lifecycle of ticket creation.

message: Operation 'searchtickets' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, poor_ai_interpretability. Original summary and description are too vague. 'Full text search' doesn't clarify what fields are searched or what parameters are needed. Enhanced version specifies searchable fields, mentions optional filters, clarifies the response structure (TicketSearchResult), and explains use cases. This significantly improves AI agent interpretability for determining when and how to use search functionality.

data.current_description: Full text search across all tickets.
data.description_suggestion: Performs full-text search across all ticket fields (subject, description, updates) to find matching tickets. Accepts a search query string and optional filters for status, priority, or date range. Returns a TicketSearchResult containing matching tickets with relevance information and pagination details. Useful for locating specific tickets by keyword or filtering by criteria.
data.reasoning: Original summary and description are too vague. 'Full text search' doesn't clarify what fields are searched or what parameters are needed. Enhanced version specifies searchable fields, mentions optional filters, clarifies the response structure (TicketSearchResult), and explains use cases. data.current_summary: List all updates for a ticket
data.current_description: Retrieves all updates associated with a specified ticket.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves all TicketUpdate objects associated with a specified ticket, ordered chronologically. Requires the ticket ID as a parameter. Returns a list of updates including author information, timestamps, content, and update type (comment, status change, assignment, etc.). Useful for viewing the complete history and conversation thread of a ticket.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and actionable.
